roles.98: triggering alert: EID13 registry SET of Environment\UserInitMprLogonScript = C:\Users\...\Temp\art.bat — logon-script persistence (T1037.001)
roles.79: REG.exe ADD that configures the UserInitMprLogonScript value

metadata.security_proposition: The configured UserInitMprLogonScript fired within the retained post-write Sysmon window.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: MISSING. A UserInitMprLogonScript value is configured, creating an alarming autostart setup. No Sysmon-observable userinit.exe/art.bat process execution appears after the write in the retained window. The package does not claim whether a logon occurred because the scoped sensor is Sysmon-only. Over-triage-prone (down-rank). Cross-corpus OTRF missing (pairs LOGON-001). Do not leak.

boundary_definition: A0 = the EID13 UserInitMprLogonScript set; EV = the reg.exe REG ADD that wrote it.
No Sysmon ProcessCreate for userinit.exe launching art.bat, or for art.bat itself, is present after the write in the retained source. A logon event itself is not claimed because this is Sysmon-only.

absence_audit.confirmation: post-write Sysmon ProcessCreate for userinit.exe launching art.bat, or execution of art.bat
absence_audit.adequacy_note: Approximately 5.2 seconds of Sysmon telemetry follows the value set; the claim is limited to no firing being evidenced in that retained window.

curation_notes.note: MISSING / over-triage-prone: logon-script setup is visible, while Sysmon-observable script firing is not evidenced in the retained post-write window.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
